steps.title: Identify the structure after the noun
steps.content: The sentence reads: “comprises some 1,200 languages [BLANK] including the Nauruan and Marshallese, which are spoken by 6,000 and 55,000 speakers, respectively, and accounts for...” The blank asks what punctuation, if any, should follow “languages.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for noun modifiers
steps.content: ”Including the Nauruan and Marshallese” is a prepositional phrase that directly follows “languages” and provides examples. When a prepositional phrase immediately modifies a noun, no punctuation separates them. Choices A (em dash), B (colon), and D (comma) all insert punctuation that would either create unwanted breaks or separate the noun from its modifier. That matches choice C — C is correct.
